channel.title: Football on The Tradewinds Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Football on The Tradewinds Brief

item.title: Election Season Training Camp: Promises FC vs Delivery United
item.description: Pre-Season Friendly. Promises FC and Delivery United line up for another exhibition match. The scoreboard says 0-0. The crowd says enough talk. The referee is not sure which side to call.

item.title: Caribbean Brief: Jamaica's Hurricane Budget, T&T Radar Gone, Guyana Plays Football & The US Removes Military Gear from Tobago
item.description: Jamaica tables its Hurricane Melissa budget, the US quietly removes military radar from Tobago, Guyana faces Belize in football, and Antigua is hosting the Commonwealth summit in November. Monday Caribbean.

item.title: Sunday's Guyana Brief - Hotels Multiply, Health Goes Digital, and Cricket Women Keep Winning
item.description: Another US$18M hotel opens, your medical records go online, President Ali says cash grants aren’t sustainable (after promising one), Women’s cricket squad stays undefeated, and Fruta Conquerors lose their president after 11 months.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Responds: Hotels Mean JOBS, Women Cricketers Making Us PROUD, and Stop Complaining!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Queens celebrates the Women’s T20 squad, defends the hotel boom, explains why digital health records are PROGRESS, and asks why the Brief can’t just be happy for once.
